Knowledge Base

Security Overview

Mnemosyne is designed with security and privacy as first-class concerns.

Security Model


flowchart TD
  A[Agent] -->|Local API| M[Mnemosyne]
  M -->|Encrypted| DB[(SQLite DB)]
  M -->|HTTPS| E[Embeddings API]
  DB -->|Backup| S3[Encrypted Backup]
  
  style M fill:#e0f2fe,stroke:#0284c7
  style DB fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#16a34a

Threat Model

ThreatMitigation
Data theft (local)Encryption at rest
Data theft (network)No network exposure by default
Prompt injectionInput validation, parameterized queries
Memory poisoningImportance scoring, confidence thresholds
Backup exposureEncrypted backups

Security Features

  • Encryption at rest: AES-256-GCM for database files
  • No cloud required: All data stays local by default
  • Input sanitization: All user inputs are parameterized
  • Audit logging: Optional access logging
  • Backup encryption: GPG or AES-encrypted backups

Compliance

StandardStatusNotes
GDPRCompatibleData stays local, no third-party sharing
HIPAACompatibleEncryption + access controls
SOC 2CompatibleAudit logs + backup procedures
Embedding API Calls

By default, Mnemosyne sends text to OpenAI for embedding generation. Use local embeddings (local_embeddings=True) to prevent any data leaving your machine.
